behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural translation output quality (grammar/meaning preservation), which stubs cannot validate. This is a single model decision path, not a multi-agent sequence, so Agentic not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run real source sentences through the production translation API and evaluate semantic fidelity + grammatical coherence against human-validated references/rubric.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff/orchestration (API adapter error -> fallback translator -> successful response). Real network/OS behavior is not required by the behavior text; a controlled 503 from the adapter is sufficient. Rejected System because the behavior does not require validating real external HTTP contract beyond handling a 503 condition.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client stub (returns 503) + SQLite-backed dictionary translator; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans fallback translator + real SQLite persistence/read path. Unit tests of lookup logic alone can miss schema/query/wiring issues. No external network/subprocess/LLM dependency, so not System/Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Seed SQLite with known phrase pairs, invoke fallback translator through service layer, and assert exact stored translation is returned.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Even if edit-distance math is unit-tested, this behavior also requires persistence wiring (compute -> metric store). No real external infra or model-quality dependence is required to validate this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Provide deterministic primary/fallback strings through service path, verify stored metric equals expected character-level edit distance in the metrics store.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is async orchestration/state handoff across local components (primary result precedence + background metric logging). Rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/subprocess/OS semantics; in-process async plus real local store readback can verify outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators enabled, assert immediate response equals primary translation, then await background task completion and verify quality metric/log entry exists.
